abstract: The evolution of fifth-generation (5G) cellular wireless networks is driven by the demand for significantly faster data rates, superior end-user quality-of-experience (QoE), ultra-low latency, and improved energy efficiency. This article explores the core emerging technologies that are poised to define the 5G landscape and shape future communication standards. Furthermore, we examine the critical research hurdles introduced by these innovations and address the complex measurement and testing challenges essential for the practical deployment of 5G systems.
